commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Moonshot's provider replay policy to enable strict duplicate tool-call ID sanitization, adds focused Kimi duplicate-ID tests, and updates the Moonshot provider docs.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +108, Docs 0. Total +124 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main preserves duplicate native Kimi IDs in the sanitizer but Moonshot's replay policy opts out of running that sanitizer, so duplicate replay IDs can reach Moonshot unchanged. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can preserve the PR's intended Moonshot chat-completions fix while restoring the existing non-target model API policy behavior.

target.title: fix(moonshot): rewrite duplicate native Kimi tool_call ids on replay
target.body:
## Summary

- Resolve the Moonshot/Kimi duplicate `tool_call_id` HTTP 400 (#51593) by opting Moonshot into the shared occurrence-aware replay sanitiser, **without** adding any Kimi-specific public policy surface — the fix shape Codex review on this PR recommended.
- `extensions/moonshot/index.ts` overrides `buildReplayPolicy` with `sanitizeToolCallIds: true, toolCallIdMode: "strict"`. The shared sanitiser already keeps native Kimi ids intact on first use (`sanitizeToolCallId` returns `functions.<name>:<index>` verbatim in strict mode, satisfying #62319) and rewrites later duplicates via the occurrence seed (`functions.exec:26` → `functionsexec262`), so the duplicate that previously reached Moonshot now gets a distinct id.
- Remove the `preserveNativeKimiToolCallIds` flag an earlier revision threaded through `ProviderReplayPolicy`, `TranscriptPolicy`, the embedded-runner replay sanitiser, and the image sanitiser. Reusing the existing native-id detector keeps a single source of truth and avoids a compatibility-sensitive plugin-facing knob (Codex P1 `src/plugins/types.ts`), and keeps `TranscriptPolicy` literals assignable (Codex P2 `transcript-policy.ts`).
- Out of scope: relaxing the #62319 invariant (native Kimi ids must survive *first* occurrence) and changing tool_call_id generation upstream of the runner.

## Linked context

Closes #51593

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request. The issue is `bug:crash`, `P1`, `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`, `queueable-fix`, `source-repro`. ClawSweeper's Codex review confirmed the bug is reproducible and that reusing the existing occurrence-aware sanitiser (rather than a new flag) is the right fix shape; this revision implements exactly that.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Moonshot/Kimi returns native tool_call_ids shaped like `functions.exec:26`. Because #62319 requires those native ids to survive replay, Moonshot disabled the replay sanitiser entirely (`sanitizeToolCallIds: false`). Long sessions / WhatsApp group chats reused the same `<name>:<index>` slot, replayed the same id twice in one request, and Moonshot rejected the second assistant turn with HTTP 400 `tool call id <id> is duplicated`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node `v22.22.3`, on top of latest `main` (which carries the refreshed prompt snapshots).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Replayed two assistant turns sharing the same `functions.exec:26` id through the real `sanitizeToolCallIdsForCloudCodeAssist` in strict mode with no Kimi-specific option — the exact transform Moonshot's `buildReplayPolicy` now drives.
- Evidence after fix: Console output / stdout captured live in the terminal by running `node --import tsx kimi-smoke.mjs` against the real `sanitizeToolCallIdsForCloudCodeAssist` and the registered Moonshot replay policy (`extensions/moonshot/index.ts`), whose `buildReplayPolicy` resolves to `{ validateGeminiTurns: true, validateAnthropicTurns: true, sanitizeToolCallIds: true, toolCallIdMode: "strict" }`. Replaying the duplicate `functions.exec:26` through the real sanitiser produced this terminal output (verbatim stdout):

  ```
  === AFTER-FIX (no Kimi-specific flag, strict mode) ===
  first  toolCall id : functions.exec:26
  first  toolResult  : functions.exec:26
  second toolCall id : functionsexec262
  second toolResult  : functionsexec262
  first preserved native : true
  second rewritten unique: true
  first pair matches  : true
  second pair matches : true
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The first `functions.exec:26` survives verbatim (so #62319 stays fixed), the duplicate gets `functionsexec262` (unique, alphanumeric via the existing `makeUniqueToolId` strict path), and the paired `toolResult` ids follow by encounter order. The exact wire shape Moonshot would reject is no longer producible.
- What was not tested: A live WhatsApp group chat against Moonshot `kimi-k2.5/k2.6`. The fix sits at the runtime replay sanitiser boundary that produces the wire-level ids, so the smoke exercises the exact transformation the live request consumes; this environment has no Moonshot credentials.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Boundary smoke against the real sanitiser, not a live Moonshot call. The reporter's HTTP 400 capture in #51593 documents the upstream side of the same boundary.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tool-call-id.test.ts extensions/moonshot/index.test.ts` → all green (tool-call-id 25, moonshot 5).
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json` and `-p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json` → no errors (the earlier `TranscriptPolicy` required-field type regression is gone).
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <touched files>` → clean; `oxfmt --check <touched files>` → all formatted.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/agents/tool-call-id.test.ts`: "preserves the first native Kimi tool_call id and rewrites duplicates" now asserts the behaviour with **no** Kimi flag — locking both the #62319 first-occurrence invariant and the dedup-on-replay fix.
- `extensions/moonshot/index.test.ts`: asserts the replay policy is `sanitizeToolCallIds: true, toolCallIdMode: "strict"` and explicitly `not.toHaveProperty("preserveNativeKimiToolCallIds")`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

On current main Moonshot's policy has `sanitizeToolCallIds: false`, so the sanitiser never runs and duplicate native ids reach Moonshot verbatim — exactly the reported HTTP 400.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes (positive). Moonshot multi-turn sessions / group chats now survive duplicate native id replays instead of failing with HTTP 400.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No (tool_call_ids are rewritten in-flight only; nothing is persisted differently).

What is the highest-risk area?

The Moonshot opt-in flips its replay policy from `sanitizeToolCallIds: false` to `true`. Native Kimi ids are still preserved on first use by the shared sanitiser, so #62319 is unaffected; only genuine duplicates change.

How is that risk mitigated?

No public policy surface is added — the change is contained to the Moonshot plugin's `buildReplayPolicy` plus the existing shared sanitiser. The `tool-call-id` test locks the first-occurrence-preserved / duplicate-rewritten behaviour so future contributors cannot regress #62319.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer / ClawSweeper review. This revision implements ClawSweeper's recommended fix shape (drop the public Kimi flag, keep Moonshot's sanitiser opt-in, preserve first native ids via the existing detector). A live Moonshot group-chat smoke would confirm the rewritten shape end-to-end.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

A live WhatsApp group-chat / long-session Moonshot smoke (needs Moonshot credentials not available in this environment). The `Real behavior proof` gate may need a maintainer `proof:` override for this boundary-level fix.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Codex review (needs changes): removed the `preserveNativeKimiToolCallIds` `ProviderReplayPolicy`/`TranscriptPolicy` surface (P1), kept Moonshot's sanitiser opt-in, preserved first native Kimi ids via the existing detector, kept transcript-policy types assignable (P2), and updated the focused tool-call-id / Moonshot tests.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the touched code paths, implemented the review-recommended fix shape, and ran the validation commands above.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
target.author: pluviobyte

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
